reasoning: The Lean statement `f '' Set.univ ⊆ closure (f '' s)` asserts $f(X) \subseteq \overline{f(E)}$, which is exactly the definition of "$f(E)$ is dense in $f(X)$" (since $f(E) \subseteq f(X)$, density in the subspace topology on $f(X)$ is equivalent to every point of $f(X)$ lying in the closure of $f(E)$ in $Y$).
